title: RWE Is Said Near Deal to Boost Stake in €10 Billion Grid Amprion
summary: German energy company RWE AG is nearing a deal to boost its stake in Amprion by buying stakes from Swiss Life and a German pension fund as part of a deal that could value the transmission grid at about €10 billion ($11.5 billion), according to people familiar with the matter.

summary: Denmark’s months-long probe into a series of drone incursions over the Nordic country confirmed the sightings were real, but has not determined who was behind.

summary: Billions of dollars rest on how quickly the crucial waterway for oil can be fully re-opened after the Iran war triggered the biggest cut in production in history.

title: Stocks Slide as Iran Nuclear Talks Hit Early Snag: Markets Wrap
summary: Global stocks are ending a strong week on a cautious note as relief over an interim peace deal between the US and Iran gave way to a focus on the challenges of securing a lasting agreement.

title: Oil Steadies as Traders Eye Lebanon Ceasefire, Hormuz Clues
summary: Oil steadied as Iran asserted its authority over the Strait of Hormuz, while Israel and Hezbollah reportedly agreed to a ceasefire starting Friday.

title: Russia Sells Stake in Seized Top Gold Miner for $1.3 Billion
summary: Russia sold a top gold miner that it seized from a billionaire last year, though only after multiple attempts and for about half the price it originally sought.

title: India Rate Setters See Outlook Improve as Middle East Risks Ease
summary: India’s rate setters chose to stay growth-supportive and hold off on any policy pivot, viewing Middle East uncertainty as a reason for caution while expecting the economic outlook to improve rapidly once tensions ease.

title: ‘Take Risk’: Canada Regulator Cuts Bank Capital Level to Boost Lending
summary: Canada’s financial regulator lowered capital requirements for the country’s largest banks for the first time in three years, giving them flexibility to lend more to support a domestic push for defense spending, critical infrastructure and artificial intelligence.

title: India Stock Regulator Lets Firms Buy Back Shares From Market
summary: India’s securities regulator will reintroduce open-market buybacks for companies to support share prices in a market that has lagged global peers.

title: Vitol Won Emergency Zambia Fuel Supply Deal That IMF Questioned
summary: Vitol Group is supplying diesel to Zambia via exclusive access to a pipeline that lasts through September, in an emergency arrangement the International Monetary Fund has urged authorities to end.

title: Gold Set for Weekly Loss on Hawkish Fed and Peace Deal Jitters
summary: Gold dropped, heading for a third weekly loss as traders weighed delays in negotiations over a permanent Middle East peace deal and the outlook for US interest rates.

title: Ambani’s Jio Could Be India’s Biggest IPO
summary: Jio Platforms is gearing up for what could become India’s biggest IPO ever. The telecom giant serves nearly 525 million subscribers and counts Meta, Alphabet and KKR among its backers. Now, Ambani’s digital crown jewel is preparing for its next chapter. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ambani’s Jio Platforms Files For Long-Awaited India IPO
summary: Jio Platforms Ltd., a crown jewel of billionaire Mukesh Ambani’s oil-to-retail conglomerate, filed draft listing documents on Friday, starting the process of unlocking shareholder value in one of India’s most-anticipated initial public offerings.

title: Andy Burnham Win Puts UK PM Under Pressure & US-Iran Talks Called Off | The Pulse 6/19/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Mark Haefele, UBS Global Wealth Management, CIO; Laurel Rapp, Chatham House, US and North America Programme Director; Jane Green, University of Oxford, Politics Professor. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran War Fuels Renewables Interest, Multilateral Lender Says
summary: The conflict in Iran, which has sent crude prices to multi-year highs, is fueling interest in renewables and projects aimed at boosting energy security, according to the Private Infrastructure Development Group.

title: Czech Billionaire Premier Feuds With Central Bank Over Rate Hike
summary: The Czech prime minister heightened his public criticism of the central bank’s tight monetary policy, saying its latest move to raise interest rates will hurt the economy.

title: The Dollar Is Riding High Thanks to a Newly Hawkish Fed
summary: Greenback’s strength is weighing on emerging-market currencies

title: UK Bonds Fall as Burnham Win Leaves Markets Speculating on Risks
summary: The UK’s government bonds fell after Andy Burnham’s victory in a special election renewed political uncertainty, prompting investors to demand a higher premium to hold the country’s debt.

title: World Cup Mints Big Prediction Winners and Million-Dollar Losers
summary: The World Cup is turning prediction markets into a public ledger of million-dollar bets, exposing big wins and dramatic losses as the tournament attracts more money to the fast-growing industry.

title: Vance Delays Iran Talks, Andy Burnham's Victory | The Opening Trade 6/19/2026
summary:
Talks between the US and Iran that had been planned for Friday in Switzerland will not take place, Reuters reported, citing a Swiss Foreign Ministry statement. Vice President JD Vance will not depart Thursday night for the face-to-face negotiations, the White House had said earlier.

The pound edged lower as Andy Burnham won a historic contest for an open parliamentary seat, paving the way for the Greater Manchester mayor to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer for his job.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Lizzy Burden. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lane Says It’s Hard to Argue ECB Shouldn’t Have Hiked Rates
summary: It’s difficult to argue that the European Central Bank should have refrained from raising borrowing costs this month, according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

title: Santander Surpasses Inditex as Spain’s Most Valuable Company
summary: Banco Santander SA has become Spain’s most valuable listed company, overtaking Zara owner Inditex SA for the first time in eight years.

title: Indian Banks Line Up $2.5 Billion Debt Sales Using RBI Swap
summary: At least four lenders including the State Bank of India are preparing to raise about $2.5 billion selling bonds in the coming weeks, tapping a Reserve Bank of India facility that makes it cheaper to borrow dollars, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: US Stocks Draw Record Weekly Inflows as Investors Pile Into Tech
summary: US stocks attracted record inflows last week as investors piled into the technology sector, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

title: Collapsed Lender MFS Said to Have Never Registered £300 Million of Mortgages
summary: A £300 million ($396 million) portfolio of mortgages linked to collapsed lender Market Financial Solutions Ltd. looks to never have been registered with public property records, according to court filings, threatening to complicate lenders’ efforts to establish their claims over the underlying assets.

title: Goldman Axes $500 From Gold Target on No Fed Cuts This Year
summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. cut its year-end gold forecast by $500 an ounce as the Federal Reserve is no longer seen easing in 2026.

title: MSCI Report Eases Fears of Indonesia Frontier-Market Downgrade
summary: MSCI Inc.’s latest review of Indonesia’s market accessibility signaled to many investors that the risk of a downgrade to frontier-market status remains low, despite the index provider’s concerns over transparency and information flow in the world’s worst-performing stock market.

title: Kenya Parliament Cuts Treasury Tax Measures to $763 Million
summary: Kenyan lawmakers watered down a slew of tax proposals fronted by the Treasury that would have raised nearly $1 billion for the East African nation’s budget.

title: Seth Klarman on Counter Narratives
summary: Barry sits down with Munger disciple Seth Klarman, CEO of Baupost Group, a Boston-based investment manager with a multi-strategy approach. They discuss Seth's start as a 25 year-old and journey to CEO. They also discuss his approach to risk, IPOs, and sectors along with his sports passions including a smaller ownership in the Boston Red Sox, horse racing, and his feelings about the Boston Celtics' 2026 season. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Candidates Deploy Army of Poll Watchers in Colombia’s High-Stakes Election
summary: Colombia’s presidential campaigns are both flagging the risk of fraud in Sunday’s election, and taking steps to prevent it.

title: US Launches Tariff Probe Into Germany Over Pharma Pricing
summary: The US launched a tariff investigation into Germany over what it called “persistent underpayment for innovative pharmaceutical products,” setting the stage for possible levies on its goods in retaliation.
